wheat.advice: Yellow rust spreads fast in cool, humid weather. Remove and destroy heavily infected leaves, improve field drainage, and use a rust-resistant wheat variety next season. Consult your local agriculture extension office before applying any fungicide, and always follow the dose printed on the product label.
wheat.recommended_action: Contact local agriculture extension officer within 48 hours; avoid excess nitrogen fertilizer until resolved.

wheat.advice: Powdery mildew thrives in dense, poorly-ventilated crops. Reduce plant density next season, avoid overhead irrigation late in the day, and remove infected debris after harvest.
wheat.recommended_action: Monitor twice weekly; escalate to extension officer if spread exceeds 25% of the field.

wheat.advice: Aphids can be reduced using ladybird beetles (a natural predator) and by avoiding excess nitrogen. Sticky yellow traps near field edges help monitor populations.
wheat.recommended_action: Introduce natural predators; re-check population after 7 days.

rice.advice: Rice blast spreads quickly under high humidity and dense planting. Improve field drainage, avoid excess nitrogen, and use certified disease-free seed next season.
rice.recommended_action: Contact local agriculture extension officer; consider resistant variety for next sowing.

rice.advice: Avoid overhead irrigation, drain standing water periodically, and balance nitrogen application. Remove and burn severely infected plants to limit spread.
rice.recommended_action: Escalate to extension officer if more than 10% of field is affected.

cotton.advice: Spread mainly by whiteflies. Remove and destroy infected plants early, control whitefly populations with yellow sticky traps, and use virus-resistant cotton varieties next season.
cotton.recommended_action: Contact extension officer; monitor whitefly population weekly.

cotton.advice: Use pheromone traps to monitor adult moth activity, practice crop rotation, and remove crop residue after harvest to break the pest life cycle.
cotton.recommended_action: Set pheromone traps; report high trap counts to extension officer.

tomato.advice: Remove infected lower leaves, avoid wetting foliage during irrigation, and rotate crops away from tomato/potato family for at least one season.
tomato.recommended_action: Monitor weekly; improve airflow by pruning lower leaves.

tomato.advice: Use pheromone traps and encourage natural predators like parasitic wasps. Remove and destroy damaged fruit promptly to prevent the pest completing its life cycle.
tomato.recommended_action: Set up traps; hand-pick visibly infested fruit.
